heading: Competitive acquisition of certain items and services
label: 1395w–3 Competitive acquisition of certain items and services

Construction of 2015 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–10, title V, § 522(b)(2)129 Stat. 177Apr. 16, 2015“Nothing in the amendment made by paragraph (1) [amending this section] shall be construed as affecting the authority of the Secretary of Health and Human Services to require State licensure of an entity under the Medicare competitive acquisition program under section 1847 of the Social Security Act () before the date of the enactment of this Act [].”42 U.S.C. 1395w–3Apr. 16, 2015

Non-Application of Medicare Fee Schedule Adjustments for Wheelchair Accessories and Seat and Back Cushions When Furnished in Connection With Complex Rehabilitative Manual Wheelchairs, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–94, div. N, title I, § 106(b)133 Stat. 3101Dec. 20, 2019“(1).—In generalNotwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary of Health and Human Services shall not, during the period beginning on , and ending on , use information on the payment determined under the competitive acquisition programs under section 1847 of the Social Security Act () to adjust the payment amount that would otherwise be recognized under section 1834(a)(1)(B)(ii) of such Act () for wheelchair accessories (including seating systems) and seat and back cushions when furnished in connection with complex rehabilitative manual wheelchairs (as determined by the Secretary), and certain manual wheelchairs (identified, as of , by HCPCS codes E1235, E1236, E1237, E1238, and K0008 or any successor to such codes).January 1, 2020June 30, 2021October 1, 201842 U.S.C. 1395w–342 U.S.C. 1395m(a)(1)(B)(ii)“(2).—ImplementationNotwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary may implement this subsection by program instruction or otherwise.”

Implementation of 2018 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–123, div. E, title IV, § 50414(c)132 Stat. 223Feb. 9, 2018“(1).—ImplementationNotwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary of Health and Human Services may implement the provisions of, and amendments made by, this section [amending this section] by program instruction or otherwise.“(2).—Non-application of the paperwork reduction actChapter 35 of title 44, United States Code (commonly referred to as the ‘Paperwork Reduction Act of 1995’), shall not apply to this section or the amendments made by this section.”

GAO Report on Impact of Competitive Acquisition on Suppliers, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–173, title III, § 302(b)(3)117 Stat. 2230Pub. L. 110–275, title I, § 154(c)(1)122 Stat. 2565Dec. 8, 2003July 15, 2008“(A).—StudyThe Comptroller General of the United States shall conduct a study on the impact of competitive acquisition of durable medical equipment under section 1847 of the Social Security Act [], as amended by paragraph (1) and as amended by section 2 of the Medicare DMEPOS Competitive Acquisition Reform Act of 2008 [probably should refer to section 154 of the Medicare Improvements for Patients and Providers Act of 2008, ], on suppliers and manufacturers of such equipment and on patients. Such study shall specifically examine the impact of such competitive acquisition on access to, and quality of, such equipment and service related to such equipment and the topics specified in subparagraph (C).42 U.S.C. 1395w–3Pub. L. 110–275“(B).—ReportNot later than 1 year after the first date that payments are made under section 1847 of the Social Security Act, the Comptroller General shall submit to Congress a report on the study conducted under subparagraph (A) and shall include in the report such recommendations as the Comptroller General determines appropriate.“(C).—TopicsThe topics specified in this subparagraph, for the study under subparagraph (A) concerning the competitive acquisition program, are the following:“(i)Beneficiary access to items and services under the program, including the impact on such access of awarding contracts to bidders that—“(I)did not have a physical presence in an area where they received a contract; or“(II)had no previous experience providing the product category they were contracted to provide.“(ii)Beneficiary satisfaction with the program and cost savings to beneficiaries under the program.“(iii)Costs to suppliers of participating in the program and recommendations about ways to reduce those costs without compromising quality standards or savings to the Medicare program.“(iv)Impact of the program on small business suppliers.“(v)Analysis of the impact on utilization of different items and services paid within the same Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System (HCPCS) code.“(vi)Costs to the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, including payments made to contractors, for administering the program compared with administration of a fee schedule, in comparison with the relative savings of the program.“(vii)Impact on access, Medicare spending, and beneficiary spending of any difference in treatment for diabetic testing supplies depending on how such supplies are furnished.“(viii)Such other topics as the Comptroller General determines to be appropriate.”

Report on Activities of Suppliers, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–173, title III, § 302(e)117 Stat. 2233Pub. L. 110–275, title I, § 154(c)(2)(C)122 Stat. 2566Dec. 8, 2003July 15, 2008“The Inspector General of the Department of Health and Human Services shall conduct a study to determine the extent to which (if any) suppliers of covered items of durable medical equipment that are subject to the competitive acquisition program under section 1847 of the Social Security Act [], as amended by subsection (a) [probably should be (b)(1)], are soliciting physicians to prescribe certain brands or modes of delivery of covered items based on profitability. Not later than , the Inspector General shall submit to Congress a report on such study.”42 U.S.C. 1395w–3July 1, 2011

Study by GAO, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–33, title IV, § 4319(c)111 Stat. 394Aug. 5, 1997“The Comptroller of the United States shall study the effectiveness of the establishment of competitive acquisition areas under section 1847(a) of the Social Security Act [], as added by this section.”42 U.S.C. 1395w–3(a)

children.heading: Establishment of competitive acquisition programs
children.label: a Establishment of competitive acquisition programs

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish and implement programs under which competitive acquisition areas are established throughout the United States for contract award purposes for the furnishing under this part of competitively priced items and services (described in paragraph (2)) for which payment is made under this part. Such areas may differ for different items and services.

children.label: I
children.text: 10 of the largest metropolitan statistical areas in 2007;

children.label: II
children.text: an additional 91 of the largest metropolitan statistical areas in 2011; and

children.label: III
children.text: additional areas after 2011 (or, in the case of national mail order for items and services, after 2010); and

children.label: ii
children.text: may be phased in first among the highest cost and highest volume items and services or those items and services that the Secretary determines have the largest savings potential.

children.heading: Waiver of certain provisions
children.label: C Waiver of certain provisions
children.text: In carrying out the programs, the Secretary may waive such provisions of the Federal Acquisition Regulation as are necessary for the efficient implementation of this section, other than provisions relating to confidentiality of information and such other provisions as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Changes in competitive acquisition programs
children.label: D Changes in competitive acquisition programs

children.heading: Round 1 of competitive acquisition program
children.label: i Round 1 of competitive acquisition program
I
children.text: the contracts awarded under this section before , are terminated, no payment shall be made under this subchapter on or after , based on such a contract, and, to the extent that any damages may be applicable as a result of the termination of such contracts, such damages shall be payable from the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund under ;July 15, 2008July 15, 2008section 1395t of this title

children.label: II
children.text: the Secretary shall conduct the competition for such round in a manner so that it occurs in 2009 with respect to the same items and services and the same areas, except as provided in subclauses (III) and (IV);

children.label: III
children.text: the Secretary shall exclude Puerto Rico so that such round of competition covers 9, instead of 10, of the largest metropolitan statistical areas; and

children.label: IV
children.text: there shall be excluded negative pressure wound therapy items and services.

children.heading: Round 2 of competitive acquisition program
children.label: ii Round 2 of competitive acquisition program
I
children.text: the metropolitan statistical areas to be included shall be those metropolitan statistical areas selected by the Secretary for such round as of ;June 1, 2008

children.label: II
children.text: the Secretary shall include the next 21 largest metropolitan statistical areas by total population (after those selected under subclause (I)) for such round; and

children.label: III
children.text: the Secretary may subdivide metropolitan statistical areas with populations (based upon the most recent data from the Census Bureau) of at least 8,000,000 into separate areas for competitive acquisition purposes.

children.heading: Exclusion of certain areas in subsequent rounds of competitive acquisition programs
children.label: iii Exclusion of certain areas in subsequent rounds of competitive acquisition programs
I
children.text: Rural areas.

children.label: II
children.text: Metropolitan statistical areas not selected under round 1 or round 2 with a population of less than 250,000.

children.label: III
children.text: Areas with a low population density within a metropolitan statistical area that is otherwise selected, as determined for purposes of paragraph (3)(A).

children.heading: Verification by OIG
children.label: E Verification by OIG
children.text: The Inspector General of the Department of Health and Human Services shall, through post-award audit, survey, or otherwise, assess the process used by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services to conduct competitive bidding and subsequent pricing determinations under this section that are the basis for pivotal bid amounts and single payment amounts for items and services in competitive bidding areas under rounds 1 and 2 of the competitive acquisition programs under this section and may continue to verify such calculations for subsequent rounds of such programs.

children.heading: Supplier feedback on missing financial documentation
children.label: F Supplier feedback on missing financial documentation
I
children.text: shall provide, by not later than 45 days (in the case of the first round of the competitive acquisition programs as described in subparagraph (B)(i)(I)) or 90 days (in the case of a subsequent round of such programs) after the covered document review date, for notice to the bidder of all such documents that are missing as of the covered document review date; and

children.label: II
children.text: may not reject the bid on the basis that any covered document is missing or has not been submitted on a timely basis, if all such missing documents identified in the notice provided to the bidder under subclause (I) are submitted to the Secretary not later than 10 business days after the date of such notice.

children.label: I
children.text: the date that is 30 days before the final date specified by the Secretary for submission of bids under such program; or

children.label: II
children.text: the date that is 30 days after the first date specified by the Secretary for submission of bids under such program.

children.label: I
children.text: applies only to the timely submission of covered documents;

children.label: II
children.text: does not apply to any determination as to the accuracy or completeness of covered documents submitted or whether such documents meet applicable requirements;

children.label: III
children.text: shall not prevent the Secretary from rejecting a bid based on any basis not described in clause (i)(II); and

children.label: IV
children.text: shall not be construed as permitting a bidder to change bidding amounts or to make other changes in a bid submission.

children.heading: Covered document defined
children.label: iv Covered document defined
children.text: In this subparagraph, the term “covered document” means a financial, tax, or other document required to be submitted by a bidder as part of an original bid submission under a competitive acquisition program in order to meet required financial standards. Such term does not include other documents, such as the bid itself or accreditation documentation.

children.heading: Requiring bid bonds for bidding entities
children.label: G Requiring bid bonds for bidding entities
children.text: With respect to rounds of competitions beginning under this subsection for contracts beginning not earlier than , and not later than , an entity may not submit a bid for a competitive acquisition area unless, as of the deadline for bid submission, the entity has obtained (and provided the Secretary with proof of having obtained) a bid surety bond (in this paragraph referred to as a “bid bond”) in a form specified by the Secretary consistent with subparagraph (H) and in an amount that is not less than $50,000 and not more than $100,000 for each competitive acquisition area in which the entity submits the bid.January 1, 2017January 1, 2019

children.heading: Treatment of bid bonds submitted
children.label: H Treatment of bid bonds submitted

children.heading: For bidders that submit bids at or below the median and are offered but do not accept the contract
children.label: i For bidders that submit bids at or below the median and are offered but do not accept the contract
I
children.text: the entity’s composite bid for such product category and area was at or below the median composite bid rate for all bidding entities included in the calculation of the single payment amounts for such product category and area; and

children.label: II
children.text: the entity does not accept the contract offered for such product category and area,

children.heading: Treatment of other bidders
children.label: ii Treatment of other bidders
children.text: In the case of a bidding entity for any product category for a competitive acquisition area, if the entity does not meet the bid forfeiture conditions in subclauses (I) and (II) of clause (i) for any product category for such area, the bid bond submitted by such entity for such area shall be returned within 90 days of the public announcement of the contract suppliers for such area.

children.heading: Durable medical equipment and medical supplies
children.label: A Durable medical equipment and medical supplies
children.text: Covered items (as defined in ) for which payment would otherwise be made under , including items used in infusion and drugs (other than inhalation drugs) and supplies used in conjunction with durable medical equipment, but excluding class III devices under the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act [ et seq.], excluding certain complex rehabilitative power wheelchairs recognized by the Secretary as classified within group 3 or higher, complex rehabilitative manual wheelchairs (as determined by the Secretary), and certain manual wheelchairs (identified, as of , by HCPCS codes E1235, E1236, E1237, E1238, and K0008 or any successor to such codes) (and related accessories when furnished in connection with such complex rehabilitative power wheelchairs, complex rehabilitative manual wheelchairs, and certain manual wheelchairs), and excluding drugs and biologicals described in section 1395u()(1)(D) of this title.section 1395m(a)(13) of this titlesection 1395m(a) of this title21 U.S.C. 301October 1, 2018o

children.heading: Other equipment and supplies
children.label: B Other equipment and supplies
children.text: Items and services described in , other than parenteral nutrients, equipment, and supplies.section 1395u(s)(2)(D) of this title

children.heading: Off-the-shelf orthotics
children.label: C Off-the-shelf orthotics
children.text: Orthotics described in for which payment would otherwise be made under which require minimal self-adjustment for appropriate use and do not require expertise in trimming, bending, molding, assembling, or customizing to fit to the individual.section 1395x(s)(9) of this titlesection 1395m(h) of this title

children.heading: Lymphedema compression treatment items
children.label: D Lymphedema compression treatment items
children.text: Lymphedema compression treatment items (as defined in ) for which payment would otherwise be made under .section 1395x(mmm) of this titlesection 1395m(z) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: rural areas and areas with low population density within urban areas that are not competitive, unless there is a significant national market through mail order for a particular item or service; and

children.label: B
children.text: items and services for which the application of competitive acquisition is not likely to result in significant savings.

children.heading: Special rule for certain rented items of durable medical equipment and oxygen
children.label: 4 Special rule for certain rented items of durable medical equipment and oxygen
children.text: In the case of a covered item for which payment is made on a rental basis under and in the case of payment for oxygen under , the Secretary shall establish a process by which rental agreements for the covered items and supply arrangements with oxygen suppliers entered into before the application of the competitive acquisition program under this section for the item may be continued notwithstanding this section. In the case of any such continuation, the supplier involved shall provide for appropriate servicing and replacement, as required under .section 1395m(a) of this titlesection 1395m(a)(5) of this titlesection 1395m(a) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: With respect to items or services included within a particular HCPCS code, the Secretary may establish a process for certain items and services under which a physician may prescribe a particular brand or mode of delivery of an item or service within such code if the physician determines that use of the particular item or service would avoid an adverse medical outcome on the individual, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: No effect on payment amount
children.label: B No effect on payment amount
children.text: A prescription under subparagraph (A) shall not affect the amount of payment otherwise applicable for the item or service under the code involved.

children.heading: Application
children.label: 6 Application
children.text: For each competitive acquisition area in which the program is implemented under this subsection with respect to items and services, the payment basis determined under the competition conducted under subsection (b) shall be substituted for the payment basis otherwise applied under , , or , as appropriate.section 1395m(a) of this titlesection 1395m(h) of this titlesection 1395u(s) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: by a physician or other practitioner (as defined by the Secretary) to the physician’s or practitioner’s own patients as part of the physician’s or practitioner’s professional service; or

children.label: ii
children.text: by a hospital to the hospital’s own patients during an admission or on the date of discharge.

children.label: i
children.text: that are furnished by a hospital to the hospital’s own patients during an admission or on the date of discharge; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to which such programs would not apply, as specified by the Secretary, if furnished by a physician to the physician’s own patients as part of the physician’s professional service.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct a competition among entities supplying items and services described in subsection (a)(2) for each competitive acquisition area in which the program is implemented under subsection (a) with respect to such items and services.

children.label: i
children.text: The entity meets applicable quality standards specified by the Secretary under .section 1395m(a)(20) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: The entity meets applicable financial standards specified by the Secretary, taking into account the needs of small providers.

children.label: iii
children.text: The total amounts to be paid to contractors in a competitive acquisition area are expected to be less than the total amounts that would otherwise be paid.

children.label: iv
children.text: Access of individuals to a choice of multiple suppliers in the area is maintained.

children.label: v
children.text: The entity meets applicable State licensure requirements.

children.heading: Timely implementation of program
children.label: B Timely implementation of program
children.text: Any delay in the implementation of quality standards under or delay in the receipt of advice from the program oversight committee established under subsection (c) shall not delay the implementation of the competitive acquisition program under this section.section 1395m(a)(20) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A contract entered into with an entity under the competition conducted pursuant to paragraph (1) is subject to terms and conditions that the Secretary may specify.

children.heading: Term of contracts
children.label: B Term of contracts
children.text: The Secretary shall recompete contracts under this section not less often than once every 3 years.

children.label: I
children.text: each subcontracting relationship that such supplier has in furnishing items and services under the contract; and

children.label: II
children.text: whether each such subcontractor meets the requirement of , if applicable to such subcontractor.section 1395m(a)(20)(F)(i) of this title

children.heading: Subsequent disclosure
children.label: ii Subsequent disclosure
children.text: Not later than 10 days after such a supplier subsequently enters into a subcontracting relationship described in clause (i)(II), such supplier shall disclose to the Secretary, in such form and manner, the information described in subclauses (I) and (II) of clause (i).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may limit the number of contractors in a competitive acquisition area to the number needed to meet projected demand for items and services covered under the contracts. In awarding contracts, the Secretary shall take into account the ability of bidding entities to furnish items or services in sufficient quantities to meet the anticipated needs of individuals for such items or services in the geographic area covered under the contract on a timely basis.

children.heading: Multiple winners
children.label: B Multiple winners
children.text: The Secretary shall award contracts to multiple entities submitting bids in each area for an item or service.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Payment under this part for competitively priced items and services described in subsection (a)(2) shall be based on bids submitted and accepted under this section for such items and services. Based on such bids the Secretary shall determine a single payment amount for each item or service in each competitive acquisition area.

children.heading: Application of coinsurance
children.label: i Application of coinsurance
children.text: Payment under this section for items and services shall be in an amount equal to 80 percent of the payment basis described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Application of deductible
children.label: ii Application of deductible
children.text: Before applying clause (i), the individual shall be required to meet the deductible described in section 1395(b) of this title.l

children.heading: Payment on assignment-related basis
children.label: C Payment on assignment-related basis
children.text: Payment for any item or service furnished by the entity may only be made under this section on an assignment-related basis.

children.heading: Construction
children.label: D Construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as precluding the use of an advanced beneficiary notice with respect to a competitively priced item and service.

children.label: i
children.text: the contractor has submitted a bid for such items and services under this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary has awarded a contract to the contractor for such items and services under this section.

children.heading: Bid defined
children.label: B Bid defined
children.text: In this section, the term “bid” means an offer to furnish an item or service for a particular price and time period that includes, where appropriate, any services that are attendant to the furnishing of the item or service.

children.heading: Rules for mergers and acquisitions
children.label: C Rules for mergers and acquisitions
children.text: In applying subparagraph (A) to a contractor, the contractor shall include a successor entity in the case of a merger or acquisition, if the successor entity assumes such contract along with any liabilities that may have occurred thereunder.

children.heading: Protection of small suppliers
children.label: D Protection of small suppliers
children.text: In developing procedures relating to bids and the awarding of contracts under this section, the Secretary shall take appropriate steps to ensure that small suppliers of items and services have an opportunity to be considered for participation in the program under this section.

children.heading: Consideration in determining categories for bids
children.label: 7 Consideration in determining categories for bids
children.text: The Secretary may consider the clinical efficiency and value of specific items within codes, including whether some items have a greater therapeutic advantage to individuals.

children.heading: Authority to contract for education, monitoring, outreach, and complaint services
children.label: 8 Authority to contract for education, monitoring, outreach, and complaint services
children.text: The Secretary may enter into contracts with appropriate entities to address complaints from individuals who receive items and services from an entity with a contract under this section and to conduct appropriate education of and outreach to such individuals and monitoring quality of services with respect to the program.

children.heading: Authority to contract for implementation
children.label: 9 Authority to contract for implementation
children.text: The Secretary may contract with appropriate entities to implement the competitive bidding program under this section.

children.heading: Special rule in case of competition for diabetic testing strips
children.label: 10 Special rule in case of competition for diabetic testing strips

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: With respect to the competitive acquisition program for diabetic testing strips conducted after the first round of the competitive acquisition programs, if an entity does not demonstrate to the Secretary that its bid covers types of diabetic testing strip products that, in the aggregate and taking into account volume for the different products, cover 50 percent (or such higher percentage as the Secretary may specify) of all such types of products, the Secretary shall reject such bid. With respect to bids to furnish such types of products on or after , the volume for such types of products shall be determined by the Secretary through the use of multiple sources of data (from mail order and non-mail order Medicare markets), including market-based data measuring sales of diabetic testing strip products that are not exclusively sold by a single retailer from such markets.January 1, 2019

children.heading: Study of types of testing strip products
children.label: B Study of types of testing strip products
children.text: Before 2011, the Inspector General of the Department of Health and Human Services shall conduct a study to determine the types of diabetic testing strip products by volume that could be used to make determinations pursuant to subparagraph (A) for the first competition under the competitive acquisition program described in such subparagraph and submit to the Secretary a report on the results of the study. The Inspector General shall also conduct such a study and submit such a report before the Secretary conducts a subsequent competitive acquistion program described in subparagraph (A).2So in original. Probably should be “acquisition”.2

children.heading: Demonstration of ability to furnish types of diabetic testing strip products
children.label: C Demonstration of ability to furnish types of diabetic testing strip products
i
children.text: demonstrate to the Secretary, through letters of intent with manufacturers, wholesalers, or other suppliers, or other evidence as the Secretary may specify, such ability; or

children.label: ii
children.text: demonstrate to the Secretary that it made a good faith attempt to obtain such a letter of intent or such other evidence.

children.heading: Use of unlisted types in calculation of percentage
children.label: D Use of unlisted types in calculation of percentage
children.text: With respect to bids to furnish diabetic testing strip products on or after , in determining under subparagraph (A) whether a bid submitted by an entity under such subparagraph covers 50 percent (or such higher percentage as the Secretary may specify) of all types of diabetic testing strip products, the Secretary may not attribute a percentage to types of diabetic testing strip products that the Secretary does not identify by brand, model, and market share volume.January 1, 2019

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of an entity that is furnishing diabetic testing strip products on or after , under a contract entered into under the competition conducted pursuant to paragraph (1), the Secretary shall establish a process to monitor, on an ongoing basis, the extent to which such entity continues to cover the product types included in the entity’s bid.January 1, 2019

children.heading: Termination
children.label: ii Termination
children.text: If the Secretary determines that an entity described in clause (i) fails to maintain in inventory, or otherwise maintain ready access to (through requirements, contracts, or otherwise) a type of product included in the entity’s bid, the Secretary may terminate such contract unless the Secretary finds that the failure of the entity to maintain inventory of, or ready access to, the product is the result of the discontinuation of the product by the product manufacturer, a market-wide shortage of the product, or the introduction of a newer model or version of the product in the market involved.

children.heading: Additional special rules in case of competition for diabetic testing strips
children.label: 11 Additional special rules in case of competition for diabetic testing strips

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: With respect to an entity that is furnishing diabetic testing strip products to individuals under a contract entered into under the competitive acquisition program established under this section, the entity shall furnish to each individual a brand of such products that is compatible with the home blood glucose monitor selected by the individual.

children.heading: Prohibition on influencing and incentivizing
children.label: B Prohibition on influencing and incentivizing
i
children.text: persuading, pressuring, or advising the individual to switch; or

children.label: ii
children.text: furnishing information about alternative brands to the individual where the individual has not requested such information.

children.label: I
children.text: the requirements established under subparagraphs (A) and (B);

children.label: II
children.text: the right of the individual to purchase diabetic testing strip products from another mail order supplier of such products or a retail pharmacy if the entity is not able to furnish the brand of such product that is compatible with the home blood glucose monitor selected by the individual; and

children.label: III
children.text: the right of the individual to return diabetic testing strip products furnished to the individual by the entity.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: ii Requirement
children.text: With respect to diabetic testing strip products furnished on or after the date on which the Secretary develops the standardized information under clause (i), an entity described in subparagraph (A) may not communicate directly to an individual until the entity has verbally provided the individual with such standardized information.

children.heading: Order refills
children.label: D Order refills
children.text: With respect to diabetic testing strip products furnished on or after , the Secretary shall require an entity furnishing diabetic testing strip products to an individual to contact and receive a request from the individual for such products not more than 14 days prior to dispensing a refill of such products to the individual.January 1, 2019

children.label: A
children.text: the establishment of payment amounts under paragraph (5);

children.label: B
children.text: the awarding of contracts under this section;

children.label: C
children.text: the designation of competitive acquisition areas under subsection (a)(1)(A) and the identification of areas under subsection (a)(1)(D)(iii);

children.label: D
children.text: the phased-in implementation under subsection (a)(1)(B) and implementation of subsection (a)(1)(D);

children.label: E
children.text: the selection of items and services for competitive acquisition under subsection (a)(2);

children.label: F
children.text: the bidding structure and number of contractors selected under this section; or

children.label: G
children.text: the implementation of the special rule described in paragraph (10).

children.heading: Program Advisory and Oversight Committee
children.label: c Program Advisory and Oversight Committee

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a Program Advisory and Oversight Committee (hereinafter in this section referred to as the “Committee”).

children.heading: Membership; terms
children.label: 2 Membership; terms
children.text: The Committee shall consist of such members as the Secretary may appoint who shall serve for such term as the Secretary may specify.

children.label: i
children.text: The implementation of the program under this section.

children.label: ii
children.text: The establishment of financial standards for purposes of subsection (b)(2)(A)(ii).

children.label: iii
children.text: The establishment of requirements for collection of data for the efficient management of the program.

children.label: iv
children.text: The development of proposals for efficient interaction among manufacturers, providers of services, suppliers (as defined in ), and individuals.section 1395x(d) of this title

children.label: v
children.text: The establishment of quality standards under .section 1395m(a)(20) of this title

children.heading: Additional duties
children.label: B Additional duties
children.text: The Committee shall perform such additional functions to assist the Secretary in carrying out this section as the Secretary may specify.

children.heading: Inapplicability of chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: 4 Inapplicability of chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: The provisions of chapter 10 of title 5 shall not apply.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: 5 Termination
children.text: The Committee shall terminate on .December 31, 2011

children.heading: Report
children.label: d Report
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on the programs under this section. The report shall include information on savings, reductions in cost-sharing, access to and quality of items and services, and satisfaction of individuals.July 1, 2011

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 110–275, title I, § 145(a)(1)122 Stat. 2547July 15, 2008
children.label: e Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 110–275, title I, § 145(a)(1)122 Stat. 2547July 15, 2008

children.heading: Competitive acquisition ombudsman
children.label: f Competitive acquisition ombudsman
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for a competitive acquisition ombudsman within the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services in order to respond to complaints and inquiries made by suppliers and individuals relating to the application of the competitive acquisition program under this section. The ombudsman may be within the office of the Medicare Beneficiary Ombudsman appointed under . The ombudsman shall submit to Congress an annual report on the activities under this subsection, which report shall be coordinated with the report provided under .section 1395b–9(c) of this titlesection 1395b–9(c)(2)(C) of this title
